root_cause: Overlapping ReplicaSet ownership is causing controller conflict
evidence: Pod labels match multiple active ReplicaSets: payments-rs-old, payments-rs-new
Recent controller timeline shows activity for conflicting ReplicaSets within 20 minutes
Representative conflict signal: ReplicaSet "payments-rs-new" cannot adopt Pod "payments-api-7d6c8d4fd9-km72p": Pod already has controller reference to ReplicaSet/payments-rs-old

causes.message: Multiple active ReplicaSets are reconciling the same rollout window
ReplicaSet selectors or owner metadata allow more than one controller path to claim the same Pod
ReplicaSet reconciliation becomes unstable because ownership and adoption are ambiguous
Controller rollout progress is impaired by conflicting ReplicaSet ownership

pod:payments-api-7d6c8d4fd9-km72p: Pod labels match multiple active ReplicaSets: payments-rs-old, payments-rs-new
replicaset:payments-rs-new: Also matches Pod labels during active reconciliation

likely_causes: Two ReplicaSets in the same namespace have overlapping selectors
A Pod was manually relabeled or ownerReferences were edited, so ReplicaSet adoption is now ambiguous
A rollout or copied manifest introduced a ReplicaSet selector that is broader than intended
suggested_checks: Compare ReplicaSet selectors and template labels with `kubectl get rs -o yaml`
Ensure only one active ReplicaSet selector matches the Pod labels in this namespace
Review recent rollout changes for manual selector or ownerReference edits
